events.name: MNY City Council Budget Hearing Field Trip
events.description: ​​Visit the New York City Council with Daniel! Ideally stay for at least an hour!​​I will be attending the Finance Committee's joint executive budget hearing with the Committee on Education. You can meet me beforehand at Remi Flower & Coffee (130 William St, New York, NY) at 9:15am if you want, after which we'll walk over. Otherwise, proceed directly to City Hall.​​What's the "executive budget"? Read this to understand what it is, and how it fits into the overall budget process:https://www.ibo.nyc.gov/assets/ibo/downloads/pdf/budget-guides/understandingthebudget.pdf​​Link to the meeting is here:https://legistar.council.nyc.gov/MeetingDetail.aspx?ID=1302700&GUID=C4C1334A-A398-452C-BE50-234ECD71AF6E&Options=info|&Search=​​If you are coming as part of class, this will count for "Witnessing Government."

events.name: Maximum New York June Party & Abundance Politics Accelerator
events.description: ​Come hang out with friends, hear some lightning talks about NYC city/state politics, find collaborators for your own political schemes, and have a good time!​You'll enjoy the company of MNY alumni, people from theabundance movementgenerally, and other guests.​There will be drinks and snacks!​The formal business agenda will be posted soon. It will include:​Presentations of no more than ~10 minutes​Details on the Abundance Resolution that attendees may vote on.​Updates on city and state politics!​Know before you arrive:This event will be guided by thefour rules of etiquettethat guide all Maximum New York classes, and is generally for people who mostly agree withThe Sensible Seventy.

events.name: Maximum New York May Party & Abundance Politics Accelerator
events.description: ​Come hang out with friends, hear some lightning talks about NYC city/state politics, find collaborators for your own political schemes, and have a good time!​You'll enjoy the company of MNY alumni, people from theabundance movementgenerally, and other guests.​There will be drinks and snacks!​There will be a short presentation of no more than 10 minutes, and we will go over the following:​The New York City rent regulation system, which oversees about a million housing units. It's the housing that Abundance has yet to fully embrace, and we are inRGB season.​The mayoral charter revision commission (CRC), and their recentlyreleased preliminary report. The CRC will put charter amendments directly on our November ballot, and they will be some of the most consequential votes you'll cast in a while.​Other ways to get involved and stay up-to-date with politics in NYC and NYS.​Details on the Abundance Resolution you will be able to vote on duringthe June MNY meeting!​And: a live quiz on NYC government (if you want to participate)! The winner gets a bundle of prizes!​Know before you arrive:This event will be guided by thefour rules of etiquettethat guide all Maximum New York classes, and is generally for people who mostly agree withThe Sensible Seventy.

events.name: OTWC Season 6: Part IX
events.description: ​OTWC is an NYC-based writing community for online writers to write, exchange ideas, and get inspired. Our sessions look like this:​​​​​​​​​​​​​1. ​​​Introduce yourselves and talk about what you plan to write2. ​​​Focused writing session3. ​​​Get together in small groups and discuss how the session went​​​​​​​​​​​​​Snacks and drinks will be provided!​​​​​​​​​​​​If this price is too steep for you for any reason, email us by clicking "Contact the Host" on the left and we’ll give you a pay-what-you-can ticket.​​​​​​​​​​​​​Follow us on Twitter and Instagram @otwc_nyc to stay updated on future events and announcements!​​​​​​​​​​​​​Hope to see you there 🥳

events.name: Behind the Fundraising Scene: the Making of an AI Deal - La Creme de la STEM™ x SPANVentures.
events.description: ​Ever wondered what really goes on behind the scenes in the world of venture capital? Join us for a lively VC Fireside Chat hosted by Marion from La Creme de la STEM™ . We'll be demystifying the VC industry, sharing insider tips, and answering all your burning questions in a relaxed, friendly atmosphere.​Come mingle, learn, and connect with fellow entrepreneurs, investors, and innovators. Whether you're a seasoned founder or just curious about how venture capital works, this is your chance to get the inside scoop—no suits, no jargon, just real talk and good vibes. See you there!​​ABOUT LA CREME DE LA STEM™​​​La Creme de la STEM™is the only global, private network for early-stage female founders in tech and science, offering a holistic support system that includes exclusive insights from exited entrepreneurs, vetted investors and perks from corporate partners.Applyto join!

events.name: Female Founders Unwind - La Creme de la STEM™
events.description: ​Join us for unwinding in great company during New York Tech Week! Show up as you are, honore where you are at and mingle with fellow female founders.​AGENDA:​9 - 9:30am: checkin and networking​9:30 - 10am: pitches of Founding Members of La Creme de la STEM™​Abby Rakshit, CEO and Founder atSeason​Yasaman Soroori, CEO and co-founder atConsulta​Holly Diamond, CEO and Founder atWork Onward​Saba Ahmed, co-founder at Tiny Dinner​Jennifer Dwork, co-founder atBummed​Elisabeth Bykoff, CEO and founder atBoxsy​Victoire Mandonnaud, CEO and founder ofOn My Way​10 - 10:15am: breath and relax withLizzy Palmer, CEO and co-founder ofSync N​10:15am - 11am: networking​​ABOUT LA CREME DE LA STEM™:​​​La Creme de la STEM™is the only global, private network for early-stage female founders in tech and science, offering a holistic support system that includes exclusive insights from exited entrepreneurs, vetted investors and perks from corporate partners.Applyto join!​ABOUT CITIZENS​Citizens Private Bankprovides holistic, high-touch client service to startups, venture capital, and private equity firms. We are a trusted partner to established venture capital funds & emerging managers, founders, and LP's, and corporates for Citizens Private Bank. We offer tailored financial solutions and expert guidance. With a rich legacy of supporting innovation and entrepreneurship, we specialize in providing comprehensive banking services to meet the unique needs of the innovation economy with an unprecedented customer service and support.

events.name: The Studio Experience: Building Self Trust
events.description: ​Your monthly ritual - complete with our 90-min Mental Wellness Workoutplustime for meaningful community time & exclusive product giveaways.​This event is all about building emotional resilience and deepening self-trust—learning how to regulate stress, navigate emotions with confidence, and reconnect with your inner voice in a world that often pulls you away from it.​You don’t have to be in crisis to deserve care. Whether you're therapy-curious or already deep in the work, this 90-minute experience is your invitation to pause, reflect, and remember: support is not a last resort—it’s a way forward.​🎟️ Tickets include:​Complimentary Giveaways & Discountsfrom our partners including LMNT, Novos, RoC Skincare, Earth Sugar, and Cymbiotika​Yoga Props Providedfor your comfort (mats, blocks, etc.)​🧘‍♀️ The 90-Minute Mental Wellness Workout​This signature session from Our Studio Wellness integrates yoga, therapist-led conversation, journaling, and meditation—all in one. It’s a complete mind-body reset designed to leave you feeling grounded, clear, and deeply supported.​🌿 What to Expect:​Vinyasa YogaLed by Our Studio Wellness founder,Justine Chun, this mindful movement session helps connect you to your body, ease tension, and rebuild trust in your inner knowing through intentional breath and flow.​Therapist-Guided Conversation & JournalingLed by NYC psychologistDr. Melissa Gluck, PH.D.,  this 30-minute session invites you to explore your relationship to support, worthiness, and self-trust.We’ll unpack emotional triggers, societal conditioning, and the stories we carry—and replace them with tools for navigating life from a more empowered, grounded place. You’ll:​Reflect on personal narratives around asking for help​Explore the difference between gut instinct and conditioning​Use guided journaling to reconnect with whatyouneed, not what the world expects​Walk away with three practical tools to build self-trust every day​Meditation & ResetA closing meditation to leave you with a renewed sense of calm and inner connection.​Connection SessionPost-workout, stay for meaningful conversations and community time—plus exclusive giveaways from our wellness partners.​Event Program:​9:45 am:Check-in begins​10:00 am:Program with Justine and Melissa​10:00 am: Yoga​10:40 am: Guided conversation & journaling​11:20 am: Meditation​11:30 am:Connection session​AboutOur Studio:​​Our Studio is a holistic wellness brand dedicated to prioritizing mental and physical well-being. We’re revolutionizing the mental health space by offering comprehensive solutions that integrate movement, nutrition, meditation, and therapy. By recognizing the interconnectedness of these elements, we bridge the gaps in a fragmented industry, providing a cohesive approach to wellness.

events.name: Cookbook Club featuring Chef Camille Becerra's Bright Cooking: Sustainable Eating from Farm to Kitchen
events.description: ​Celebrate Springby connecting with the earth and local food experts for an evening focused on sustainable, seasonal, local eating featuring Chef Camille Becerra. Learn food sourcing and cooking tips from farmers, chefs, and sustainability experts. After a panel talk, we'll mingle over light, farm-fresh bites from Chef Becerra's book,Bright Cooking. Put what you learned into action by volunteering to lead or planning to attend a community  Farmer’s Market Field Trip.​Our Letters from the Table ​cookbook club is a growing community of folks who love to cook and gather. If food is your love language, this is the place for you.​​​What to Expect:​This month, we’ll be cooking and sharing dishes from Camille Becerra’s cookbook,Bright Cooking.We’re honored to have Chef Becerra joining us for the evening to share her expertise and ethos for creating flavorful, beautiful, sustainable plant-based meals!​Our sustainable food panel will feature:​Camille Bercerra, Chef and Author,Bright Cooking​Claire Matern, Founder of JONI​Anina von Haeften, Co-founder of Farm to People​Jess Vetterli, Founder of Simply Sustainable​Moderator: Sonia Ketkar, Co-Founder of CoSee​If you want to join a Farmers' Market Field Trip,we'll have a list of NYC farmers’ markets available so you can find the one that works best for you. Make a plan to meet up with other attendees or friends. Use it as an opportunity to shop together, sharing tips and building relationships with the farmers and local food producers at your neighborhood market.​​​Rough Agenda:​​​​​6:00pm:Gather. Grab a JONI Verjus Mocktail or N/A wine from Third Place Bar to Sip.6:30pm:Learn from the Experts in our Sustainable Food Panel.7:15pm:Learn Fresh Techniques for Seasonal Cooking in a Cooking Demo Led by Our Host, Joni, using Recipes fromBright Cookingand highlighting Produce from Farm to People.7:45pm:Share a Potluck Meal of Fresh Bites fromBright Cooking. Mingle.8:30pm:Make a Local, Seasonal Food Shopping Plan. Find a Friend for a Farmer’s Market Field Trip Before You Leave.​​​How to Prepare:​​Sign up to attend​​Select a recipe from the featured cookbook:Bright Cooking​​Check out a copy from your local library, buy a copy, or find one of the recipes from the book online.​​If you aren't able to get the cookbook and cook ahead of time, pick up something to add to the table that fits the theme.​ Store-bought is fine.​Add the Element or Dish you plan to bring to therecipe sign up sheetalong with any dietary notes. Dishes are listed in the 2nd tab. Our goal will be to create a giant grazing table.​Bring your contribution ready to share. A microwave is available for reheating your dish if needed.​We'll have plates, cups, and silverware on hand.​​​​Meet Our Featured Guests & Partners:​Chef Camille BecerraCELEBRATED CHEF & TASTEMAKER WITH DEDICATED FOLLOWING: Camille Becerra is an “it” girl in the food world, both for her dishes and her style in and out of the kitchen. Featured in the New York Times Style Magazine, Bon Appetit, Vogue, Refinery 29, Elle, Condé Nast Traveler, Martha Stewart Living, Kinfolk, and on the cover of Cherry Bombe, to name just a few, her cooking and food styling are iconic—instantly recognizable, covetable, gorgeous, and packed with nutrients.​Anina von Haeften,Farm to PeopleAnina is Co-Founder and Creative Director of Farm To People. Farm To People delivers the farmer’s market straight to your doorstep in NYC. Their mission is to make it easier to eat well at home by curating farm shares and a marketplace with the best seasonal, local produce, sustainable meat and fish, and ethical grocery staples. Experience Farm to People by placing your order from their website or visiting their beautiful restaurant and event space in Bushwick.​Jess Vetterli,​Simply SustainableJess is the founder of Simply Sustainable, an education platform and community on sustainable living. Her love of the environment came from her upbringing in California where she spent a lot of time outside and in nature. After studying climate change in college, she became committed to life-long involvement in climate action. Her career in climate includes being a sustainable seafood educator at the Monterey Bay Aquarium, management consulting for environmental orgs, business development for a SaaS climate startup, and founding a zero-waste CPG business. She started Simply Sustainable to provide the tools, resources and community that she wishes she had when she started her journey. It's built on the belief that individuals have the power to change the world for the better. Jess will also be pursuing a Masters in Climate and Society at Columbia this fall.​Claire Matern,JONIJONI celebrates verjus. Verjus is pronounced vair-joo. Think "vert jus" in French. And it means just that. Verjus translates to green juice. It's the juice of the grapes that are picked before harvest. Vines are culled about 6 weeks before a wine harvest to make some elbow room for the grapes that will be wine to stretch out, plump up, and get those sugars activated. The pre-harvest grapes traditionally have been pressed and enjoyed as a drink and used in cooking.​Claire grew up in the food industry in New York City. Her family owned a cheese and specialty food store for 20 years and her mission has always been to take the “overwhelm” out of cooking, take food and ingredients off a pedestal, and make cooking inspiring and something you celebrate getting to do rather than feeling like it’s something you have to do.​​Sonia Ketkar,CoSeeSonia is a data product consultant who leads the development of tech-driven solutions for corporate clients. She also writes a newsletter and hosts a podcast, both titled 'Where Data meets Business'. Personally, she has a passion for sustainability and deep interest in the role of food supply chains in climate and health. Previously, she launched a platform for helping consumers make local and green food choices from buying and storing food to disposing it. Currently, she is Co-Founder of CoSee, a soon to be launched space for sustainable and responsible food and other products.​Third Place BarThird Place Bar NYCis a series of alcohol-free pop-up bar events in New York City founded by Sam Bail in late 2022. The goal of Third Place Bar is to provide an environment for people to socialize and connect without feeling the pressure to drink - whether they’re just not drinking for the night, taking some time off, or are on a longer alcohol-free journey.​​Meet Your Hosts:​​​​​Letters from the TableLetters from the Tableis a collection of love letters to people, places & times that changed us. It is a place where sensory memories are expressed through recipes. Most importantly, it is an invitation to the table — to experience intensely personal perspectives expressed through food and to share your own.Sign up for the newsletterto receive periodic letters, recipes, and atmospheric content.​We host our cookbook club events as members of theCookbook Club NYcommunity, which Stephh Lau started to spread the joy of hosting cookbook clubs. Join herDiscordgroup to learn about other Cookbook Club chapters and events!​Joni GoldbachJoni, the creator of Letters from the Table, is a fractional Product leader supporting business leaders and teams in reaching their goals and creating meaningful experiences for their customers. She is passionate about finding and creating moments of connection in her work and through passion projects like Letters from the Table.​Johnny KamalJohnnyis a Lebanese data engineer who immigrated to the US thirteen years ago and grew up in a food-forward household, so cooking was his best way to stay in touch with his culture. He is determined to bring happiness to as many people as possible one bake at a time, and is determined to bring people from different cultures and backgrounds together through our one common language: Food.​FabrikWelcome toFabrik! We are on a mission to uplift communities and foster serendipitous connections in real life.​​​​Fabrik is a home for communities where everyone has a place to belong. With spaces designed to feel more like your living room than your office, Fabrik’s third spaces are vibrant hubs where people can come together in real life, explore interests, forge meaningful connections, and build a sense of community.​​​If you'd like to come early and work for the day,day passes are available for $29 here. And if you'd like to become a member, useReferral Code FROMTHETABLEat check-out tosave $49 on your 1st month of Fabrik membership.​Event FAQ​Do I have to cook and bring a dish to share?This is a potluck event designed for folks who love to cook and explore the perspectives, cultures and history shared through cookbooks, but we get it. Schedules get busy, and it's not always possible to cook something for a weekday event. In a pinch, pick up something to share that fits the theme. As Ina Garten says, "store-bought is fine"!​How do you handle allergies and food safety?Cookbook Club events are potlucks where we share a meal of home-cooked dishes together. We expect attendees to use common sense food handling and storage when preparing for the event. We have a microwave and refrigerator on site for reheating or keeping food cool, but if you are worried about food safety or storage in transit, opt for bringing a dish that is safe to store and serve at room temperature.​We do everything that we reasonably can to make sure that all who come to our events have enough to eat and don’t leave hungry. Please reach out to us ahead of time if you have food allergies or dietary restrictions we should be aware of. On our recipe signup list, we ask that folks list allergens so that other attendees can plan ahead.​Can I receive a refund for my ticket?We use RSVPs to plan for the number of attendees and ticket pricing to cover our costs in hosting. Unfortunately, we cannot grant refunds, but you are welcome to pass your ticket on to someone else.​Is there an age restriction?Our events are 21+. We will have a selection of non-alcoholic and potentially lightly alcoholic beverages (think wine and beer) on hand to accompany our shared meals and enhance the experience. The goal is social lubrication, not inebriation. We aim to provide a safe and comfortable space where folks can connect. We wiill check IDs as needed, and folks who are displaying unwelcome behavior will be asked to leave.​The artwork in the event image is amazing! Who is the artist?That'sStill-life with Quince, Cabbage, Melon and Cucumberby Juan Sánchez Cotán (c. 1600) at the San Diego Museum of Art. Gorgeous, right?! The artist is known for establishing the bodegón style of still life exemplified here. If you're interested in learning more about the artist and trying a recipe inspired by his work,read this article from art historian Dr. Megan Fizell.​Where can I find more events like this?Feeling the love? There are lots of other events coming up to keep those good vibes going.​Keep an eye on theFabrik calendarfor more upcoming gatherings in Tribeca and Dumbo.If you are free on Saturday, May 17th, join the Fabrik community in DUMBO for a Special Saturday Adventure.Follow Letters from the Tableon substackandon Instagramto be the first to know about our future events.​And… to find more Cookbook Club Chapters in the local NY/NJ community and beyond, sign up for Stephh’s Cookbook Club CommunityDiscord! We’re just one of many fantastic local groups.​​​​Please note: By signing up for this event you agree to receive email communication from us and from Fabrik related to this gathering and future offerings. You can unsubscribe at any time. Photos will be taken during the event for promotional purposes. By attending, you consent to your image being used in future marketing materials. If you have any concerns, please let us know.

events.name: Third Place Bar x Shanti House Mindful Friday Night Social
events.description: ​Third Place Bar & Shanti Houseinvite to you a different kind of Friday night out. Join us at the new Fabrik location in Dumbo for a relaxing and slowfirst timers yoga class(aimed at anyone who isn’t familiar with yoga), light snacks, a tea bar byMOOSA, non-alcoholic drinks and a live DJ to wind down and de-stress from the week... without the hangover.​What to expect:​6.30pm-7pm: Arrivals & mingling(please make sure to arrive on time before the yoga session!)​7pm-7.45pm: Unwind yin yoga led byShanti House​7.45pm-8.30pm: Social hour with NA drinks & snacks​Limited spots available. Our last Friday Night Social sold out a week before the event date, so please make sure to grab your ticket early!​FAQ​​Q: Do I need to be there at the exact start time?Yes, please aim to arrive at 6.30pm as we will begin the yoga class at 7pm and latecomers may not be admitted!​​​Q: Can I come alone?Yes, this is a social mixer, you’re welcome to come alone and meet new people!​​​Q: Is this an alcohol-free venue?Yes, Fabrik is a community space that does not serve alcohol.​​​Q: Is there an age restriction?Yes, all our events are 21+​Q: I've never done yoga, can I still come?Yes! This yoga class is specifically aimed at folks who haven’t done yoga and aren’t familiar with the poses. We’ll keep the poses simple and walk you through them!​Q: Do I need to bring a mat? Can I change somewhere?Yoga mat rental is included in the ticket price, but you're welcome to bring your own! There are limited changing rooms available.​Q: I need to cancel, can I get a refund for my ticket?Unfortunately, we cannot offer refunds, but you're welcome to pass on your ticket to someone else.​​​Please note: By signing up for this event you agree to receive email communication from us. You can opt out any time.

events.name: WHF - Work From Hekate (May Mondays)
events.description: ​Join us for a relaxed Monday afternoon coworking session at Hekate, the OG sober bar in the East Village. Sam, the founder of Third Place Bar, will be there to work and welcome you.​Hekate is a sober barthat offers tea, coffee (drip), cold brew, and a full menu of alcohol-free cocktails, functional drinks, beer & soda. And of course, they have free wifi and outlets.​Bring your laptop and a project to work on, or maybe just grab a book and get some reading done.​Please note that Hekate is a bar andnot suitable for calls- if you have phone/zoom calls to make, this session might not be suitable for you!​🎟️ PRICEThis event is free, but we ask you to order minimum 1 drink from Hekate Café to support the venue while using their space.​⏰SCHEDULE2pm - 5pm:Focused co-working with quick intros at the beginning.

events.name: 🎨 Steep & Sketch: A Tea Picnic & Art Session 🍵
events.description: ​🎨 Steep & Sketch: A Tea Picnic & Art Session 🍵​📍 Lower Manhattan (Outdoors Park)​(Exact location shared after RSVP)​🗓️ May 18th – 2-4 PM | Limited Spots​💵 $20 Early Bird (RSVP by May 15)​💵 $30 Regular (after May 15)​(Includes teas, snacks, and all art supplies!) 🎁​Join us for a relaxed afternoon of sipping tea and making art in a picnic-style setting. We’ll enjoy a selection of teas (hot and cold), and spend some time sketching, painting, or simply enjoying the atmosphere. Light snacks will be provided, and art supplies will be available.​Any levels are welcome!​If you’re new to drawing or painting, casual guidance and creative prompts will be available if you'd like a little inspiration.​🌿 Please note: We’ll be sitting on picnic blankets. If you're uncomfortable sitting on the ground for extended periods, feel free to bring a small folding stool or cushion!​​Q&A! 🎨🍵​👤 Who is this event for?​Anyone curious about drawing or painting​Tea lovers looking for a slow afternoon of creativity​Those craving a mindful break in the middle of NYC​​🎁 What’s included?​✔️ A curated Tateaco tea tasting (green, black, and herbal)​✔️ Cold brew and hot options​✔️ Light snacks​✔️ Drawing + painting supplies (limited—feel free to bring your favorite tools or sketchbook)​✔️ A cozy creative setting with optional tips and prompts​​💡 Important Info​Spots are limited – RSVP to hold your place 💛​Tickets are non-refundable, but if you can’t make it, you can transfer your ticket to a friend or receive a Tateaco store credit of equal value.​This event may be photographed or filmed. If you prefer not to be included in any media, just let us know at the start of the event.​*Weather permitting. In case of rain, the event will be postponed to a later date.

events.name: AAPI Gen Z Founder + Creator Pop-Up
events.description: ​AAPI Gen Z Founder + Creator Pop-up​Hosted at Kiehl's Flagship Store​Friday, May 16th, 2025 | 3:00-4:30pm​Event Overview​Join us for an exclusive afternoon showcasing the best AAPI Gen Z founders and creators! This curated marketplace event brings together innovative Asian American and Pacific Islander entrepreneurs (and non-AAPI supporters) all under one roof at the iconic Kiehl's Flagship Store.​Experience products, meet the visionaries behind emerging brands, and connect with a community of forward-thinking entrepreneurs and creators. Perfect for networking, discovering new brands, and capturing content in beautiful natural lighting.​About GenZtea​GenZtea is a community platform connecting diverse Gen Z founders, entrepreneurs, and creators through exclusive events, networking opportunities, and resource sharing. Founded with the mission of amplifying underrepresented voices in business, GenZtea hosts regular mixers, panels, and coworking events that help emerging entrepreneurs build meaningful connections, gain visibility, and access mentorship opportunities.​About Foundess​Foundess is an accelerator initiative for female and non-binary Gen Z entrepreneurs, providing early-stage support, funding connections, and business development resources. Through our targeted programming, we help women and non-binary founders navigate the unique challenges they face in the startup ecosystem while building a supportive community of peers and mentors.​About StoryTell​StoryTellis a gathering to build in-person connection through vulnerability, courage, and shared stories. Inspired by each event's theme, we explore our perspectives, curiosities, and personal stories to realize that we're not alone in our human experience.​About KLQUE​KLQUE is a next-gen platform built for creators, founders. We seek to help people turn ideas into movements, turning storytelling and influence into real growth. Think of it as the creative engine for tomorrow’s brands—part strategy studio, part AI lab, and fully community-led. If you’re building a brand, a voice, or a vision, KLQUE is where your momentum starts.​About Wicked Pay​Wicked Pay is a fintech startup on a mission to help the next generation feel confident and in control of their money. Through its AI-powered app, Wicked Pay gives Gen Z the tools to track spending, cut waste, build savings habits, and get clear on their finances — all without stress or judgment. But Wicked Pay is more than just an app — it’s a growing community of financially curious and freedom-seeking young people, coming together to learn, share, and build a smarter relationship with money.​Featured Collaboration: NYC Tech Week Female Founders Series A+ Event​GenZtea and Foundess are proud to present our exclusive NYC Tech Week event for female founders who have raised Series A or $1M+ at Isshiki Matcha. This premier networking and resource-sharing initiative is generously sponsored by Citizens Bank, Morpheus, and Lowenstein Sandler.​All AAPI Pop-up attendees are welcome to join our Female Founders event:https://partiful.com/e/W0Cg0SbD39bbKt5FrXZk​What to Expect​Meet AAPI-owned brands and their founders​Sample exclusive AAPI-owned products​Network with fellow entrepreneurs and creators​Free skin consultation + hydration check by Kiehl's employees​Featured Brand​Isshiki Matcha- At Isshiki Matcha, their mission is to bring the serene beauty and timeless tradition of Japanese matcha to the bustling streets of New York City and beyond. Rooted in a commitment to quality, authenticity, and sustainability. At Isshiki Matcha, they believe that every cup is more than just a beverage - it's a ritual, a sanctuary, a reflection of our shared commitment to living with intention and harmony.​Sponsorship Opportunities​Interested in sponsoring this event? We offer various partnership packages that connect your brand with influential Gen Z founders and creators. For sponsorship inquiries, please contact: natalie@thegenztea.com​Location​Kiehl's Flagship Store​Space is limited. Please RSVP to secure your spot.

events.name: NYC Tech Week Prayer Walk
events.description: ​NYC Tech Week Prayer Walk​A Co-hosted Event by  GenZtea, Tech Walk, and Christians in Tech​Join us for a moment of mindfulness before the hustle of NYC Tech Week​Start your NYC Tech Week experience with intention and community. Join fellow tech professionals, entrepreneurs, and innovators for a guided prayer walk along Manhattan's iconic High Line park.​Event Details​Date:Saturday, June 1st, 2025Time:3:00 PM ESTMeeting Point:Shake Shack at Hudson YardsEnd Point:Chelsea Market​What to Expect​Our journey will begin with a group prayer at Hudson Yards, followed by a reflective walk along the elevated High Line park. As we stroll through this urban oasis, we'll have the opportunity to connect, share intentions for the week ahead, and enjoy breathtaking views of the city.​We'll conclude our walk at Chelsea Market with a final prayer of gratitude and intention-setting for NYC Tech Week.​Who This Is For​This event focuses on Christian founders and investors in the tech community, creating space for faith-centered networking and reflection. However, we warmly welcome participants of all backgrounds and beliefs who are interested in starting NYC Tech Week with intentionality and community.​Why Join?​Center yourself before the busy conference schedule​Network with like-minded professionals in a relaxed setting​Experience a unique perspective of NYC​Set meaningful intentions for your Tech Week experience​What to Bring​Comfortable walking shoes​Water bottle​Open mind and heart​This event is open to all, regardless of faith background or tradition. Our focus is on community, mindfulness, and setting positive intentions for the week ahead.​Register now to reserve your spot!​-----------------------------------------------------------------​About the Organizers​GenZteais a community of Gen Z founders, creators,and investors that brings Gen Z not just in the room, but at the table.​Christians in Techwas founded by Andy Choi to create community and support for Christians working in the technology sector. The organization focuses on integrating faith with professional life in the tech industry.​TechWalkwas created by Christine Farrier Rosemin as a platform for meaningful connection in the tech community through guided walks that combine networking with mindfulness and movement.

events.name: aci-d club weekly hang 024 *we go on hiatus after this one*
events.description: ​LUMA FEELS LIKE THE WRONG PLATFORM FOR A PARTY. RSVP HERE:​https://partiful.com/e/ObvFx4T0HtKySwrACWEB​-​You know what they say... the best time to join aci-d club was 23 weeks ago. the second best time is now. the third best time is in like three months when we're back.Join us for the last aci-d club hang before the weekly hangs go on hiatus for the summer (we’ll still be having the odd field trip or workshop here n there though — stay tuned).​No coworking this time around, just hanging out. We’ll have a-pou’s catered and perhaps some drinks, though we’d appreciate if y’all BYOB or any other snacks.​Whether you’ve attended multiple times, or just once, we appreciate you being part of the journey nonetheless.​::)

events.name: aci-d trip : Brutalist Architecture Tour
events.description: ​EDIT:We’re oversubscribed. Access will be granted as space opens up. Preference will be given to aci-d club members!​Let’s marvel at the oppressive beauty of brutalist buildings, from iconic landmarks to hidden gems. Our journey begins in Tribeca, where we’ll stroll along the west side, ending at Washington Square Park. Along the way, we’ll explore architectural wonders such as the AT&T Long Lines Building and the Salt Shed.​During our tour, we’ll delve into the history of these buildings, discussing the visionary architects behind their design, their original purposes, and how they are utilized today. We’ll also explore the challenges and triumphs faced during their construction, adding depth to our understanding of these monumental structures.​This walking tour is at street level, so be sure to wear comfortable shoes and bring along a bottle of water!Note: if you're able, paying $20 helps sustain aci-d club and makes future events possible—we’d really appreciate your support!_Liza Paganois a lifelong New Yorker and a curious Creative Director with a love for all things design. She has a fondness for wabi-sabi philosophy, brutalist architecture, and a well-worn river stone. In her spare time, you’ll find her riding her bike all over NYC, always in search of that one special block she’s never seen before—and she always finds it.

events.name: DeSciNYC: AI and Government in NYC
events.description: ​AbstractNew York is considering some of the most ambitious AI regulations in the country. Assemblymember Alex Bores, who introduced several of the bills, will explain what’s on the table and take questions in an open Q&A.​-----BioAlex Bores is a New York State Assembly Member for Manhattan's East Side, recognized for proactive AI legislation. A former data scientist at Palantir with a master's in computer science, he left due to ethical concerns over the company's ICE collaboration. Bores introduced several AI bills focused on consumer protections, data transparency, and digital content authenticity. Elected in 2022 as the first Democrat in the legislature with a computer science degree, he collaborates closely with tech companies to create effective AI policies.-----Supported ByStadium ScienceFreedom-----Stay Connected about Future EventsTwitter:@descinyc_Instagram:@descinyc​PS: Apply to be a member of the DeSci NYC group chat:https://forms.gle/AmUtotmCgWgGxWDp6
